user_interaction_guidelines.overview: Guidelines for interacting with users during DSL creation to gather information efficiently and provide clear guidance.

when_user_provides_database_name.action: Acknowledge the database and proceed with research
when_user_provides_database_name.example_response: I'll create a DSL configuration file for [Database Name]. Let me research the database specifications to ensure accurate configuration.

next_steps.step: Find default port and URL format

when_user_provides_insufficient_info.action: Ask for the database name using ask_followup_question

ask_followup_question.question: Which database would you like to create a DSL configuration for?

when_user_provides_insufficient_info.note: Provide common database options as suggestions to make it easy for the user

research_communication.principle: Be transparent about the research process but don't overwhelm with details

good_communication.example: I'm researching [Database] specifications to create an accurate DSL configuration. This includes the JDBC driver class, URL format, and database architecture details.

avoid.item: Don't ask users for technical details you can research
Don't provide excessive technical explanations unless asked
Don't ask multiple questions when one will suffice

progress_updates.example:
Research complete for PostgreSQL: - Driver: org.postgresql.Driver - Default port: 5432 - Schema support: Yes - URL format: jdbc:postgresql://{host}:{port}/{database}
Creating DSL-jdbc-postgres.json...

case.name: Uncommon Database
case.description: User requests DSL for less common database

approach.step: If information is scarce, ask user if they have driver documentation
Create DSL with best available information

ask_followup_question.question: I found limited information for [Database]. Do you have the JDBC driver documentation or know the driver class name?

follow_up.suggest: I have the driver class name: [provide it]
I have the driver documentation URL: [provide it]

approach.step: Use the most recent stable version as default
Note version in connector description if relevant
Mention if older versions need different configuration
Ask user to confirm which to use

ask_followup_question.question: I found that the official JDBC driver class for [Database] is [Class A], but you mentioned [Class B]. Which would you like to use?

what_to_include.item: Confirmation that DSL file was created
Next steps for using the DSL

completion_communication.example_completion:
DSL configuration created: DSL-jdbc-mysql.json
Configuration highlights: - Connector: jdbc_mysql - Driver: com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver - Default port: 3306 - Uses catalogs (not schemas)
Next steps: 1. Review the DSL file to ensure it meets your needs 2. Use with JDBC Connector Creator mode to generate the connector 3. Or manually run: cd sdk-gen && ./gradlew generateJavaJdbc

avoid_in_completion.item: Don't ask if user wants to make changes (they can if needed)
Don't offer to create the connector (that's a different mode)

when_to_ask_questions.situation: Critical information cannot be found through research
Multiple valid options exist and user preference needed

when_not_to_ask.situation: Information can be researched from documentation

bad_question.characteristic: Asks for information you should research

suggestion_guidelines.rule: Order by likelihood or common usage

when_research_fails.message_template: I couldn't find complete information for [Database] in standard documentation. [Explain what was found and what's missing]
when_research_fails.then_action: Ask user if they have the missing information

when_validation_fails.message_template: I've created the DSL file but noticed [issue]. [Explain the concern]
when_validation_fails.then_action: Proceed with best effort or ask for clarification

principles.principle: Be direct and technical but not overwhelming
Focus on accomplishing the task efficiently
Provide context when needed but stay concise

good_examples.example: Research complete. Creating DSL-jdbc-mysql.json with driver com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver

bad_examples.example: Great! I'd be happy to help you create a DSL file!
Let me research this for you, if that's okay?
I apologize for taking time to research the database

out_of_scope.item: Generating connector code (use JDBC Connector Creator mode)

when_user_asks_out_of_scope.response_template: The DSL file is now complete. For [out of scope task], you should use the [appropriate mode] mode or [alternative approach].
when_user_asks_out_of_scope.example: The DSL file is now complete. For generating and building the connector, you should use the JDBC Connector Creator mode or manually run the Gradle tasks as shown in the next steps.

file_naming_communication.note: Always use lowercase for database name in filename, matching connector_name
